prompt:
Read the workflow below and follow it. Task ID: GROVE-S03-T07.

---

## Prior phase summaries (carry-forward)

### plan
Objective: Implement grove doctor with a new core::harness module (sentinel constants + mode→content tables), core::doctor (pure diagnose with universal + explore checks), and Cmd::Doctor CLI verb with --json and exit-code semantics.
Verdict: n/a
Key changes: New core/src/harness.rs: GROVE_START, GROVE_END, MCP_SERVER_KEY, expected_mcp_args(Mode), expected_claude_marker(Mode), agents_md_expected(Mode) — resolves constant-ownership blocker.; cli/src/init.rs refactored: remove local CLAUDE_START/CLAUDE_END/MCP_SERVER_KEY consts; import from grove_core::harness — sentinel strings live in exactly one place.; New core/src/doctor.rs: Status{Ok,Warn,Fail,Info}, Check{group,name,status,detail,hint}, Report{mode,checks}, Report::ok(), diagnose(root,force)->Report — pure read-only.; core/src/lib.rs: add pub mod harness and pub mod doctor.; Universal checks: grove_version, config_present, legacy_explore_json, four harness sub-checks, registry_root, grammar_cache, project_languages, lock_integrity.; Harness sub-checks use harness::expected_mcp_args/expected_claude_marker/agents_md_expected; validate on-disk files vs declared mode; Fail on drift.; Explore-mode checks (McpLlm only): explore_config_valid, provider_reachable, model_served, allowed_tools_known, tap_config.; cli/src/main.rs: Cmd::Doctor{path,explore,standard}; dispatch arm; grouped ✓/⚠/✗ human output or --json; exit code from Report::ok().; Unit tests in core/src/doctor.rs build all fixtures by hand (tempdir + std::fs::write) — resolves test-strategy blocker (no cross-crate private helpers).; Harness drift matrix: per-mode clean fixture → all harness checks Ok; selected drift scenarios → correct Fail/Warn.; warn_only_report_exits_zero: Report::ok() true when only Warn/Info present — AC 9 warning boundary (per reviewer advisory).; Integration tests: doctor --help, clean project exits 0, --json parses, induced drift exits 1 under both output modes.
Full artifact: PLAN.md
### review_plan
Objective: Re-reviewed the revised grove doctor plan (core::harness + core::doctor + Cmd::Doctor) to confirm the two prior blockers and one advisory were resolved and all reused seams exist.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: Blocker 1 (constant ownership) resolved: new core/src/harness.rs is single source of truth; init.rs migrates to import it — no core->cli cycle, no duplication.; Verified on-disk init.rs owns CLAUDE_START/END (l.20-21) and MCP_SERVER_KEY (l.79); plan's per-mode markers match real steering blocks (outline/skill/explore).; Blocker 2 (cross-crate private test helpers) resolved: unit tests build tempdir fixtures via std::fs::write, no reconcile_harness/seed_lock imports.; Advisory (warn-only exits zero) incorporated as warn_only_report_exits_zero; Report::ok() correctly keys only off Status::Fail.; Seam active_mode(root, ModeChoice)->Mode confirmed (config.rs:291); ModeChoice None/ForceExplore/ForceStandard matches --explore/--standard precedence.; verify_lock (registry.rs:465), health_probe + HealthError{Unreachable,ModelMissing} (client.rs), list_models, ExploreConfig::validate, toolset consts, registry helpers all present.; Mode enum {Mcp,Skill,Both,McpLlm,Grammars} matches expected_mcp_args mapping; global --json flag present (main.rs:24) so AC6 dual-format exit code is implementable.; Advisory (non-blocking): harness_serve_surface is Info-only; acceptable since surface is derived and .mcp.json drift is already a hard Fail. No live determine_surface seam — compute inline.; Advisory (non-blocking): ensure explore/standard bool->ModeChoice conversion mirrors serve; decide+test precedence when both flags passed.
Full artifact: PLAN_REVIEW.md
### implementation
Objective: Implement grove doctor with core::harness (sentinel constants), core::doctor (pure diagnose), and Cmd::Doctor CLI verb with --json and exit-code semantics.
Verdict: n/a
Key changes: New core/src/harness.rs: GROVE_START, GROVE_END, MCP_SERVER_KEY, expected_mcp_args(Mode), expected_claude_marker(Mode), agents_md_expected(Mode) — single source of truth.; cli/src/init.rs: removed local CLAUDE_START/CLAUDE_END/MCP_SERVER_KEY consts; imported from grove_core::harness — sentinel strings in exactly one place.; New core/src/doctor.rs: Status{Ok,Warn,Fail,Info}, Check, Report, Report::ok(), diagnose(root,force)->Report — pure read-only using core primitives.; core/src/lib.rs: added pub mod harness and pub mod doctor (alphabetically ordered).; Universal checks (11): grove_version, config_present, legacy_explore_json, four harness sub-checks, registry_root, grammar_cache, project_languages, lock_integrity.; Harness sub-checks compare on-disk state to harness::expected_* functions; Fail on drift with grove init --as <mode> hint.; Explore-mode checks (McpLlm only): explore_config_valid, provider_reachable, model_served, allowed_tools_known, tap_config.; cli/src/main.rs: Cmd::Doctor{path,explore,standard}; grouped human output or --json; exit 1 from Report::ok() under both formats.; Unit tests in harness.rs: 3 coverage tests for all Mode variants across all three functions.; Unit tests in doctor.rs: warn_only exits zero, harness matrix all 5 modes, 4 drift scenarios, lock absent, explore absent, provider unreachable.; Integration tests in cli/tests/cli.rs: doctor --help, clean project exits 0, --json valid, drift exits nonzero (human + json).
Full artifact: PROGRESS.md
### code_review
Objective: Independently reviewed the grove doctor implementation (core::harness, core::doctor, Cmd::Doctor) against the approved PLAN and task ACs.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: Isolated true T07 footprint via git diff HEAD: reconcile_harness in the cumulative diff is committed T04 work, not T07.; T07 changes: new harness.rs + doctor.rs, lib.rs module wiring, init.rs constant migration (8 lines, aliased GROVE_START/END), main.rs Cmd::Doctor, cli.rs integration tests.; Verified independently: cargo clippy -D warnings clean; cargo test --release --locked green (177 core + 92 CLI unit + 33 integration + 1 doc, 0 failed).; Read actual harness matrix/drift/explore tests — assertions discriminate real Ok/Warn/Fail, not tautologies.; AC1 shapes exact; Report.mode is core declared Mode not Surface; diagnose pure/read-only.; AC2 mode resolution via shared active_mode(root, force); --explore/--standard precedence correct.; AC3 universal checks present; harness drift -> Fail naming the file with grove init --as <mode> hint.; AC4 lock_integrity delegates to T06 verify_lock: Mismatch->Fail, Missing/absent->Warn (never Fail).; AC5 explore checks gated on McpLlm; HealthError hints surfaced verbatim; allowed_tools warn on unknown.; AC6 Cmd::Doctor + --json dual-format with exit code from Report::ok(); both formats integration-tested.; AC8 build/clippy clean, --help documents doctor, both new files newline-terminated.; Advisory only: Report.mode shows declared (not forced) mode in header; steering legality covered via validate(); cosmetic double blank line in init.rs.
Full artifact: CODE_REVIEW.md
### validation
Objective: Validate all 10 acceptance criteria for grove doctor: core::harness sentinel constants, core::doctor types/diagnose, universal checks, harness drift detection, lock_integrity, explore-mode checks, Cmd::Doctor CLI verb with --json and exit-code semantics.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: AC1 PASS: core/src/harness.rs is single source of truth; cli/src/init.rs imports via grove_core::harness alias — no local duplicates.; AC2 PASS: core::doctor exports Status, Check, Report, diagnose — pure read-only verified by source inspection.; AC3 PASS: Doctor dispatch converts --explore/--standard to ModeChoice; diagnose calls active_mode(root,force) internally.; AC4 PASS: All 11 universal checks present: grove_version, config_present, legacy_explore_json, 4 harness sub-checks, registry_root, grammar_cache, project_languages, lock_integrity.; AC5 PASS: Harness checks Fail on drift with 'grove init --as <mode>' hint; 4 drift unit tests validate specific mismatch scenarios.; AC6 PASS: lock_integrity via verify_lock; absent→Warn (not Fail); Mismatch→Fail; Missing→Warn; unit test confirms boundary.; AC7 PASS: explore_checks gated on Mode::McpLlm; all 5 explore checks present; HealthError hints surfaced verbatim.; AC8 PASS: Cmd::Doctor + global --json + exit 1 from std::process::exit(1); 4 integration tests confirm both output formats.; AC9 PASS: Report::ok() is all(!Fail); warn_only_report_exits_zero unit test directly validates Warn/Info exits 0.; AC10 PASS: clippy clean; 303 tests (177+92+33+1), 0 failed; doctor_help_documents_verb confirms --help coverage.
Full artifact: VALIDATION_REPORT.md

# Collate
## Iron Laws

- Collation is a read-and-rewrite of generated markdown. Do not mutate any JSON record under `.forge/store/`; the store is the source of truth and collation flows downstream from it.
- Read `.forge/personas/collator.md` first; print the persona identity line (emoji, name, tagline) to stdout before any other tool use.
- All store reads via `forge_store` (or `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs`). Never edit `.forge/store/*.json` directly.
- Do NOT call `set-summary` or `set-bug-summary` from within collation. The collator writes markdown views and a `WRITEBACK-SUMMARY.json` disk file only. Calling `set-summary` mutates the JSON store and violates Iron Law 1 (the store is the source of truth; collation flows downstream from it, not into it). The orchestrator reads `WRITEBACK-SUMMARY.json` directly — no store write is needed.

<!-- See _fragments/store-write-verification.md — NOTE: this file uses an intentionally modified
     Store-Write Verification variant: collation typically writes markdown views (not JSON records),
     so the preamble explains when `forge_store` calls apply. Canonical fragment is reference only. -->
## Store-Write Verification

Collation typically writes markdown views, not JSON records. If a remediation
step does call `forge_store` and the call exits non-zero or the `PreToolUse`
write-boundary hook blocks the call (exit 2):

1. Parse the structured error (names the offending field + schema file).
2. Correct the JSON to satisfy the schema.
3. Retry. Repeat up to 3 times.
4. After 3 failures, halt and escalate with original payload, corrected payload, and all error messages.

Never set `FORGE_SKIP_WRITE_VALIDATION=1` — operator-only emergency switch.

## Algorithm

```
1. Preferred: Run Plugin Tool
   - Run: `node .forge/tools/collate.cjs [SPRINT_ID]`
   - If tool succeeds, proceed to Finalize

2. Fallback: Manual Collation
   - Read `.forge/config.json` for prefix, paths, project description
   - Read all sprint/task/bug/event JSONs from `.forge/store/`
   - Generate MASTER_INDEX.md (sprint registry, task registry, bug registry)
   - Generate per-sprint TIMESHEET.md (from events)
   - Generate any other configured views

3. Rebuild context pack:
   - Rebuild the architecture context pack so orchestrators have a fresh summary
     after any KB updates (architecture docs may have changed during the sprint):
     ```
     ENGINEERING=$(node .forge/tools/manage-config.cjs get paths.engineering 2>/dev/null || echo engineering)
     node .forge/tools/build-context-pack.cjs \
       --arch-dir "$ENGINEERING/architecture" \
       --out-md .forge/cache/context-pack.md \
       --out-json .forge/cache/context-pack.json
     ```
   - On exit 1 (architecture directory absent), skip silently.

4. Finalize:
   - **Do NOT emit a phase event yourself.** The orchestrator (or kickoff handler) owns event emission — it composes the canonical event from runtime telemetry (model, provider, tokens, wall times) plus the SUMMARY you write in the next step. Subagents that call `store-cli emit` for phase events hallucinate runtime facts (see Plan 11 / Slice 2). Write the SUMMARY and return.
   - Write `WRITEBACK-SUMMARY.json` to the sprint's artifact directory — use the sprint record's `path` field (read it from the store), not a reconstructed `engineering/sprints/{sprintId}/` template — with the following shape:
     ```json
     {
       "objective":   "<one sentence — what views were regenerated>",
       "key_changes": ["<up to 6 bullets — which files were written>"],
       "verdict":     "n/a",
       "written_at":  "<current ISO 8601 timestamp>"
     }
     ```
     The orchestrator reads this file directly to compose the collation event narrative. Do NOT call `set-summary` to register it — that would mutate the store in violation of Iron Law 1.
   - KB link refresh (best-effort; skip if unavailable):
     When running standalone in the Claude Code TUI, invoke via the Skill tool
     (`skill: "forge:refresh-kb-links"`).
     When running as a subagent within forge-cli (Pi runtime), the TS orchestrator
     calls `runRefreshKbLinks` directly after this phase — skip this step.
```

<!-- See _fragments/generation-instructions.md for Generation Instructions template -->

🍃 **grove Collator** — I gather what exists and arrange it into views. No AI judgement required — deterministic regeneration from the JSON store.

## Identity

I am the grove Collator. I deterministically regenerate markdown views from the JSON store. I do not make AI judgements — I invoke the generated tool or fall back to manual collation per spec.

Run this command using the Bash tool as my first action (before any file reads or other tool use):
```bash
node .forge/tools/banners.cjs drift
```
## What I Produce

- `MASTER_INDEX.md` — project-wide navigation hub
- `TIMESHEET.md` — per-sprint and per-bug time tracking
- `INDEX.md` — per-directory navigation hubs
- `COLLATION_STATE.json` — last collation metadata

## Preferred Method

Run the vendored collate tool:
```bash
node .forge/tools/collate.cjs
```

## Fallback Method

If the tool is unavailable, manually read the JSON store and produce
the same outputs following the collation algorithm in
`meta/tool-specs/collate.spec.md`.

- **Engineering root**: `engineering/architecture/`
- **Store root**: `.forge/store/`
- **Entity model**: 

## Commands

- **Syntax check**: `cargo test --release --locked`
- **Lint**: `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`

Perfect! I have successfully completed the collation workflow for GROVE-S03-T07. Here's a summary of what was accomplished:

## Collation Complete ✓

### Actions Performed

1. **Persona Identity** — Confirmed as 🍃 **grove Collator**: deterministically regenerate markdown views from the JSON store.

2. **Collate Tool** — Successfully ran `node .forge/tools/collate.cjs GROVE-S03`:
   - ✅ Regenerated `MASTER_INDEX.md` (project-wide sprint/task registry)
   - ✅ Regenerated `engineering/sprints/GROVE-S03/INDEX.md` (sprint navigation)
   - ✅ Regenerated 7 task `INDEX.md` files (per-task summaries)
   - ✅ Regenerated `COST_REPORT.md` (from events in store)

3. **Context Pack Rebuild** — Ran `build-context-pack.cjs` to refresh the architecture context pack:
   - ✅ `.forge/cache/context-pack.md` (markdown summary for orchestrators)
   - ✅ `.forge/cache/context-pack.json` (structured data)

4. **Finalize** — Wrote `WRITEBACK-SUMMARY.json` to the sprint artifact directory:
   - ✅ `engineering/sprints/GROVE-S03/WRITEBACK-SUMMARY.json` — canonical summary for orchestrator event composition

### Iron Laws Compliance

- ✅ No JSON records in `.forge/store/` were mutated — store is the source of truth
- ✅ No `set-summary` or `set-bug-summary` calls made
- ✅ All store reads via `forge_store` MCP tool
- ✅ WRITEBACK-SUMMARY.json written as disk artifact only (orchestrator reads it directly)

The collation is ready for the orchestrator to compose the completion event.
